mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in three months, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds threshold, or signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in residential real estate credit and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline by > 2% annualized or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative or Rank IC drops below 0.02 during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.3 percent month-over-month, accelerating inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or inflation threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage-driven consumption, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or payroll growth shows no significant positive correlation with quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and residential credit expansion signals unsustainable demand leverage, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses predictive significance or forward returns turn positive in > 55% of triggered divergence cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates a supply shock environment that compresses corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of stagflation episodes or Rank IC remains below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.
